oneLiner: A due-diligence assistant that turns app marketplace listings into risk reports, valuation notes, and post-acquisition product plans.
targetUsers: People buying apps on Flippa, Acquire.com, Microns, Indiemaker, and SideProjectors
Indie hackers evaluating micro-SaaS or mobile app acquisitions
problem: Marketplace buyers see asking price, revenue claims, and short descriptions, but often lack a structured way to evaluate app quality, retention risk, technical handover, store risks, and growth upside.
solution: Let users paste listing and app store information, then generate a structured risk score, diligence checklist, valuation notes, buyer questions, and a post-acquisition improvement PRD.
marketGap: Marketplaces help discovery and transactions, but they do not give every buyer a neutral app-specific risk and improvement analysis.
originalValue: The product combines marketplace diligence, app-store quality checks, review-gap mining, and PRD generation into one buyer workflow.
revenueOpportunity: App buyers can pay for individual reports or a subscription because one avoided bad deal can save far more than the report cost.
whyNow: More small apps, AI tools, and micro-SaaS products are being sold, while solo buyers need faster diligence without hiring consultants.

evidence.finding: Acquire.com, Flippa, Microns, Indiemaker, and SideProjectors list apps, SaaS, AI tools, and side projects for sale.
Due diligence tools can monetize per report because users have high purchase intent.
Buyers need app-specific technical, review, and store-risk analysis beyond generic listing data.

competitors.name: Marketplaces
competitors.weakness: Limited independent analysis for each buyer
competitors.opportunity: Provide external diligence and report generation

competitors.name: Manual consultants
competitors.weakness: Expensive and slow for small deals

goals: Help buyers reject weak deals faster.
Produce a clear diligence report from listing data.
Generate a post-acquisition product improvement PRD.
userStories: As a buyer, I want a risk score so I know whether to continue diligence.
As an indie hacker, I want buyer questions so I can contact sellers intelligently.
As an acquirer, I want an improvement PRD so I know what to build after purchase.
functionalRequirements: The app must accept manual listing details.
The app must calculate basic revenue multiples.
The app must generate a risk report.
The app must generate diligence questions.
The app must export DOCX and Markdown.
nonFunctionalRequirements: The app must avoid presenting output as legal or financial advice.
The app should preserve source URLs and date checked.
monetization: Team plan for small acquisition firms
successMetrics: At least 30 percent of users generate a second report
Paid conversion on first serious listing analysis
User reports that output changed diligence decision
validationPlan: Share sample reports with 10 app buyers.
Launch a private beta in indie acquisition communities.
risks: Paid app intelligence APIs may be costly.
openQuestions: Which marketplace should be supported first?
Should the first product target buyers or sellers?
